full_message:
============================================================
  HEALTH ADVISORY: Civil Lines
  Moderate Air Quality — Limit Prolonged Exposure
  Risk Level: Moderate | AQI: 134 | Next 24 hours
============================================================

Members of sensitive groups may experience health effects. The general public is less likely to be affected.

Time Context (immediate): Take action now to protect yourself and your family.
Population at Risk: 70,955 out of 180,000 (39.4% of ward population)

Pollutant Alert: PM10 levels are elevated. These coarse particles can irritate the eyes, nose, and throat.

Local Context: Civil Lines has a higher-than-average respiratory disease prevalence (14.5%). A significant portion (37.3%) of the population are children or elderly.

Recommended Precautions:
  1. Sensitive groups: reduce prolonged outdoor exertion
  2. Take more breaks during outdoor activities
  3. Keep windows closed during peak pollution hours
  4. Monitor symptoms if you have respiratory conditions

Vulnerable Groups Affected:
  - Children — higher breathing rate and developing lungs make them more susceptible
  - Elderly (60+) — age-related decline in lung function increases vulnerability
  - Pregnant women — air pollution affects both maternal and fetal health
  - People with asthma, COPD, or other respiratory conditions
  - People with heart disease or hypertension

--- This is an AI-generated advisory. Follow official government protocols. ---

  Unhealthy Air Quality — Take Protective Measures
  Risk Level: High | AQI: 202 | Next 24 hours
============================================================

Everyone may begin to experience health effects. Members of sensitive groups may experience more serious health effects.

  1. Avoid prolonged outdoor exertion
  2. Wear N95/KN95 masks when outdoors
  3. Keep windows and doors closed

  7. Follow medication schedules for respiratory conditions

  - Pregnant women — air pollution affects both maternal and fetal health
